steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence already has an introductory participial phrase: “finding that administrative unit GK13 had a 0.62 ratio of native to non-native trees.” After a participial phrase, the main clause must follow — and a main clause requires a finite (tensed) verb. The blank is where that finite verb must appear.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — recommended: A finite past-tense verb. With it, the sentence reads “The researchers … recommended efforts to improve the biodiversity …” — a complete independent clause. ✓
  • Choice B — recommending: A present participle. Using it gives two participial phrases in a row with no finite verb, leaving the sentence without a main clause. ✗
  • Choice C — to recommend: An infinitive. Infinitives cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice D — having recommended: A perfect participle. Like the other non-finite forms, this cannot anchor a main clause. ✗
A is correct.
